name: capture
context: Capture errors, messages, and breadcrumbs and enrich Sentry events with user context, tags, and scopes
info: This skill covers Sentry's event capture and enrichment API: captureException for caught errors, captureMessage for textual events, addBreadcrumb for contextual trails, setUser for identifying users, setTag/setContext/setExtra for structured metadata, and withScope for scoped overrides. Every error Sentry receives is enriched with breadcrumbs (a timeline of user actions, network requests, and console logs), tags (indexed key-value pairs for search/filter), context (structured data like device info or feature flags), and user identity. Agents use this skill to add manual error reporting to catch blocks, annotate events with business context, create breadcrumb trails for debugging, and identify which user hit an error. The SDK must already be initialized via [[sentry/setup]] before any capture methods are called. See [[sentry/config]] for filtering events with beforeSend and ignoreErrors.

sections.title: captureException
sections.body: Use `Sentry.captureException(error)` to capture caught errors. Pass an `Error` object for full stack traces. Optionally pass a second argument with `{ tags, extra, level, contexts, user, fingerprint }` to enrich the event inline. Non-Error values (strings, objects) are accepted but produce lower-quality events without stack traces — always prefer wrapping in `new Error()`. Returns an event ID string you can display to users for support reference.

sections.title: captureMessage
sections.body: Use `Sentry.captureMessage(message, level?)` to send textual events when there is no exception. The second argument is a severity level string: `fatal`, `error`, `warning`, `log`, `info`, or `debug`. Alternatively, pass a context object as the second argument: `{ level, tags, extra }`. Use for business logic warnings, audit events, and threshold violations.

sections.title: Breadcrumbs
sections.body: Breadcrumbs record a timeline of actions leading up to an error. The SDK auto-captures: DOM clicks, XHR/fetch requests, console.log/warn/error calls, and URL navigations. Add custom breadcrumbs with `Sentry.addBreadcrumb({ category, message, level, data })`. Categories are freeform strings for grouping (e.g., `auth`, `cart`, `api`). The SDK keeps up to `maxBreadcrumbs` (default 100) and attaches them to the next captured event. Use `beforeBreadcrumb` in [[sentry/config]] to filter or redact sensitive breadcrumbs.

sections.title: User identification and context
sections.body: Call `Sentry.setUser({ id, email, username, ip_address })` after authentication to attach user identity to all subsequent events. Use `ip_address: '{{auto}}'` for automatic IP capture. Call `Sentry.setUser(null)` on logout. Use `Sentry.setTag(key, value)` for indexed searchable data and `Sentry.setContext(name, data)` for structured non-indexed data. Use `Sentry.setExtra(key, value)` for one-off debug data. Tags are the primary filter mechanism in Sentry's issue search.

sections.title: Scopes and withScope
sections.body: Sentry has three scope levels: global (all events), isolation (per-request in servers), and current (local). `Sentry.setTag()` and `Sentry.setUser()` write to the isolation scope by default. Use `Sentry.withScope(callback)` to create a temporary scope that applies only to events captured inside the callback — useful for attaching context to a single error without affecting other events. Access scopes directly with `Sentry.getGlobalScope()`, `Sentry.getIsolationScope()`, and `Sentry.getCurrentScope()`.
